title: FORM-L Requirement Atom
description: Canonical, abstraction-neutral unit of a FORM-L requirement-atom registry. Extends the FORM-L language (Bouskela et al. 2021/2022) with WHO/ELSE/LIMIT/SIGNAL and mandatory provenance back to the raw elicitation transcript. This schema validates the YAML frontmatter of each registry/atoms/*.md file.

id.description: Stable unique identifier, e.g. RA-0001. Never reused after deprecation.

intent.description: One-line natural-language statement of the requirement. The human-readable handle; survives all rendering.

status.description: Lifecycle. 'draft' = freshly extracted, unconfirmed by client; 'confirmed' = validated with stakeholder (BA Astound Way step 3); 'superseded' = replaced by a newer atom (see relations.superseded_by).

kind.description: FORM-L distinguishes properties / requirements / assumptions / guards. 'business_rule' added for retail domain (e.g. exclusion logic).

who.description: [WHO] Actor/Role that owns or sources the intent — stakeholder, role, external system, or artifact. Extension to base FORM-L (per meta-model: 'WHO defines intent/responsibility').

where.description: [WHERE] Component, subsystem, object set, screen, or data scope the requirement applies to. Maps to ArchitecturalModel/Objects.

when.description: [WHEN] Time locator (continuous/discrete) or event trigger. Translates to ETL (Extended Temporal Language). Null when the requirement is timeless/structural.

what.description: [WHAT] The formal condition that must hold (or be avoided) — the requirement proper. Maps to a Condition.

how_well.description: [HOW_WELL] Tolerance, probability, SLO, or degradation acceptability. Maps to ProbabilisticConstraint. Often a plain threshold (e.g. '70% over 3 months') or N/A for business software.

else.description: [ELSE] Fallback / degradation path when WHAT is not met or WHERE-scope is false. Maps to Fallback/Degradation Mode.

limit.description: [LIMIT] Hard boundary / safety invariant that must NEVER be violated, regardless of mode. Maps to Hard Boundary/Invariant.

signal.description: [SIGNAL] Observable signal(s) the requirement emits or checks. Maps to Observer output.

assumptions.description: What is taken as true for this requirement to hold (FORM-L: assumptions).

guards.description: Model-validity conditions; violation means the requirement's reasoning is no longer consistent (FORM-L: guards).

observers.description: Which signals/states must be observed to evaluate this requirement (FORM-L: observers turn physical/business concepts into functional signals).

bindings.description: Where those signals come from in the real system/model (e.g. 'DWH.availability_metric', 'Cabinet AK pairs tab', 'FORM-L model var').

forml.description: Optional Modelica_Requirements-style formal expression, e.g. 'requirement RA-0001 = when orderSubmitted check paymentConfirmed within s2;'. Null when not yet formalizable.

scenarios.description: Nominal and negative scenarios; acceptance criteria are derived from these on render.

verdict_policy.description: Which evaluation statuses are blocking for release (e.g. 'negative scenario N1 must pass before go-live').

levels.description: Native BA Astound Way requirement level(s): 0 Value Chain, 1 Vision, 2 Project Scope, 3 Business Requirement, 4 Functional Requirement. Most atoms are level 4 and roll UP.

relations.description: Links to other atoms. depends_on drives the dependency map (BA Astound Way step 4).

source.description: MANDATORY provenance back to the RAW cleaned transcript (NOT the MoM). At least one entry. This is what makes the registry trustworthy and auditable.

jira.description: Optional linkage to issue-tracker keys, e.g. JIRA-123.

glossary_terms.description: Domain terms touched, validated (read-only) against your project glossary.

confidence.description: Extractor's self-assessed confidence the atom faithfully reflects the source.

extracted_by.description: Provenance of the extraction (skill or workflow name + version).
